danger_signals.meaning: Primary antagonist threat; narrative antagonist representing evil force
Hazardous entities that damage player character

safety_signals.meaning: Allied magical entity providing support and aid to protagonist

safety_signals.visual_form: Inventory-tracked vials that can be filled and used
safety_signals.meaning: Resource items for health recovery or magical effects; player agency over consumables

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Merlin character identity as wizard/magic user

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: On-screen health display/hearts system (referenced in error checking code)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player vulnerability increases as health decreases

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Inventory screen display showing collected items and vials
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can manage and deploy consumable resources during gameplay

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Vials that track fillable potion/spell resources
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Vial state changes affect available magical abilities

environmental_storytelling.technique: No printed text throughout entire game
environmental_storytelling.example: Visual and symbolic communication of narrative and mechanics without dialogue boxes or text; relies on iconography, animation, and player inference
Setting in Merlin mythology establishes magical world context; Lady of the Lake reference carries narrative weight through cultural/literary familiarity
Level design communicates progression and narrative through spatial arrangement rather than exposition

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Movement through large level spaces, interaction with objects, puzzle-solving, combat engagement
Primary evil force driving conflict; represents obstacle to player progression
Provides aid and support; suggests magical empowerment of player character

notes: Young Merlin employs a unique narrative communication strategy by eliminating printed text entirely, forcing reliance on visual iconography and player interpretation. The game emphasizes puzzle-solving and exploration over combat challenge (per reviews noting lack of difficulty). The minecart sequences represent distinct mechanical variations. Health/inventory systems are referenced in development code, indicating careful state-tracking UI. The game's approach to manifestation appears grounded in magical action-adventure convention (vials, spells, ally NPCs) rather than faction-based visual distinction. World state manifests primarily through resource availability and player status rather than environmental shifts.
